evals.name: new-project-no-docs-explore-routing
evals.prompt:
I want to add a payment checkout flow to my e-commerce app. Users should be able to add items to cart, enter credit card details, and complete purchase. We're using Stripe for payments. Can you help me build this?

<session_start_context>
routing_hint: explore
docs_exists: false
</session_start_context>
evals.expected_output: PM should: (1) acknowledge it needs to understand the project first via explore, (2) ask structured wave-based questions about the project before doing anything, (3) NOT immediately write any code or produce a plan, (4) NOT immediately create spec without asking questions

assertions.name: does_not_write_code_immediately
assertions.description: PM should NOT output any code blocks (```) in the first response — it's in CLARIFY phase

assertions.name: asks_clarifying_questions
assertions.description: PM should ask at least 2 questions about the project before proceeding

assertions.name: does_not_jump_to_implement
assertions.description: PM should NOT mention spawning implementers or creating PLAN.md in first response

assertions.name: explore_or_discuss_phase
assertions.description: PM should indicate it's in explore/discuss phase (CLARIFY), not IMPLEMENT

evals.name: direct-code-request-hard-constraint
evals.expected_output: PM should: (1) REFUSE to write the implementation code directly, (2) explain it delegates code writing to subagents, (3) offer to start the CLARIFY process OR spawn a subagent to implement it, (4) NOT produce the actual TypeScript implementation

assertions.name: refuses_to_write_implementation
assertions.description: PM should not produce the full AuthService TypeScript implementation

assertions.name: explains_delegation_principle
assertions.description: PM should mention delegation to subagent/implementer

assertions.name: offers_proper_workflow
assertions.description: PM should offer to run through proper workflow (spec or execute via subagent)

evals.name: spec-approved-transition-to-implement
evals.prompt:
The spec for user authentication is ready and approved. Please proceed with implementation.

<session_start_context>
project_root: /myapp
docs_exists: true
state_exists: true
project_exists: true
</session_start_context>

The docs/state.md currently reads:
```
## Current State
Phase: CLARIFY
Status: spec_approved
Feature: user-authentication
Spec: docs/features/user-authentication/spec.md
Approval: confirmed by user
```

The spec acceptance criteria:
- AC-001: User can register with email + password
- AC-002: User can login and receive JWT token
- AC-003: Protected routes reject invalid tokens
- AC-004: Token expires after 24 hours
evals.expected_output: PM should: (1) read state.md and recognize spec is approved, (2) transition to IMPLEMENT phase, (3) run plan action to break work into TASK-N format with waves, (4) show the plan with tasks, waves, and TEST fields before executing

assertions.name: transitions_to_implement_phase
assertions.description: PM should explicitly mention transitioning to IMPLEMENT phase

assertions.name: creates_task_plan
assertions.description: PM should produce or describe a plan with TASK-N format

assertions.name: references_acceptance_criteria
assertions.description: PM should reference AC-xxx items in its plan

assertions.name: does_not_ask_more_requirements
assertions.description: PM should NOT ask for more requirements — spec is frozen in IMPLEMENT phase

evals.name: trivial-task-direct-fix-exception
evals.prompt: There's a typo in docs/README.md line 12. It says 'recieve' but should be 'receive'. Please fix it.
evals.expected_output: PM should: (1) recognize this as a trivial < 5 line change explicitly described by user, (2) fix it directly without spawning a subagent, (3) NOT go through full CLARIFY workflow for such a simple request

assertions.name: handles_trivially_without_full_workflow
assertions.description: PM should not trigger full CLARIFY→IMPLEMENT for a single typo fix

assertions.name: acknowledges_trivial_exception
assertions.description: PM should apply the trivial exception (< 5 lines, exact change described)
